mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hiking periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, with a mean forward return below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 205 index level forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL breaches 205, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k while UNRATE falls, with a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects underlying economic growth that supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive two-month decline in industrial production signals manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances following two consecutive months of INDPRO decline.
